page.title: Mangusta 80 vs Canados 86: Which Marbella Flagship

data.meta_description: Mangusta 80 vs Canados 86 in Marbella: compare 24m vs 26m, 12 pax each, jet ski included on Mangusta, pricing & more. Book your flagship charter today.
data.summary: Compare the Mangusta 80 and Canados 86, two flagship motor yachts available for charter in Marbella. Both depart from Puerto Banús, carry 12 passengers, but differ in length, amenities, and pricing.
key_facts: Mangusta 80: 24m length, 12 pax, departs Puerto Banús.
Canados 86: 26m length, 12 pax, departs Puerto Banús.
Mangusta 80 minimum 4 hours at €4,719, includes a free jet ski.
Canados 86 pricing on request via WhatsApp.
Mangusta 80 available in grey and white variants.
Both yachts include licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and VAT.
Canados 86 offers more deck space and a larger flybridge.
Mangusta 80 is sportier and faster, ideal for thrill-seekers.

faq.q: Which is bigger, the Mangusta 80 or Canados 86?
faq.a: The Canados 86 is larger at 26 metres compared to the Mangusta 80 at 24 metres. Both accommodate 12 passengers, but the Canados offers more deck space and a larger flybridge for entertaining.

faq.q: Does the Mangusta 80 charter include a jet ski?
faq.a: Yes, every Mangusta 80 charter (including grey and white variants) includes a free jet ski. This is not included with the Canados 86, though you can request one via WhatsApp.

faq.q: How much does it cost to charter the Mangusta 80 in Marbella?
faq.a: The Mangusta 80 costs €4,719 for a minimum of 4 hours. This includes the jet ski, skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% IVA. Longer durations are available on request.

faq.q: Can I charter the Canados 86 for a half-day trip?
faq.a: Yes, half-day and full-day charters are available. Pricing is on request — simply message us on WhatsApp with your preferred dates and duration, and we'll provide a quote.

faq.q: Which yacht is faster, the Mangusta 80 or Canados 86?
faq.a: The Mangusta 80 is designed for sporty performance and higher speeds, making it a great choice if you want to cover more coastline or enjoy water sports. The Canados 86 prioritises luxury cruising and stability.

faq.q: Do both yachts depart from Puerto Banús?
faq.a: Yes, both the Mangusta 80 and Canados 86 depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella. You can also arrange pick-ups from other marinas along the Costa del Sol on request.

faq.q: What is included in the charter price for both yachts?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden extras.

faq.q: Which yacht is better for a large group celebration?
faq.a: Both are excellent for groups of up to 12. The Canados 86 offers more interior and exterior space for socialising, while the Mangusta 80 adds a jet ski for added fun. It depends on your priorities.

data.body_html:
Choosing between the Mangusta 80 and Canados 86 for your Marbella charter is a question of style, space, and speed. Both are flagship motor yachts departing from Puerto Banús, each carrying up to 12 passengers. But they differ in length, amenities, and pricing. This guide breaks down the key differences to help you decide which yacht suits your day on the Costa del Sol.

At a glance: Mangusta 80 vs Canados 86

Both yachts are available for private charter from Puerto Banús. Here's a quick comparison:

FeatureMangusta 80Canados 86
Length24 m26 m
Passengers1212
Departure marinaPuerto BanúsPuerto Banús
Minimum hire4 hoursOn request
Price from€4,719 (4h)On request
Jet ski includedYesNo (available on request)
VariantsGrey & whiteSingle spec

For a full list of yachts, see our Marbella boat rental hub.

Size and space: Canados 86 takes the lead

The Canados 86 measures 26 metres, two metres longer than the Mangusta 80's 24 metres. That extra length translates into a larger flybridge, more deck space, and a roomier saloon. If you're planning a day with a group that values lounging and socialising, the Canados offers more room to spread out. The Mangusta 80, while slightly smaller, still provides ample space for 12 guests, with a sleek, sporty profile that turns heads in the marina.

Performance and speed: Mangusta 80 is the sportier choice

The Mangusta 80 is built for performance. Its planing hull and powerful engines deliver higher cruising speeds, making it ideal for covering longer distances — say, from Puerto Banús to Estepona or even Sotogrande in less time. The Canados 86 is a semi-displacement yacht, prioritising comfort and stability over raw speed. If you want to feel the wind in your hair and enjoy water sports, the Mangusta is your pick. For a smooth, relaxed cruise along the Golden Mile, the Canados excels.

Inclusions and extras: jet ski vs. pure luxury

Every charter with us includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). The Mangusta 80 sweetens the deal with a free jet ski included in the €4,719 (4-hour) price. The Canados 86 does not include a jet ski as standard, but you can request one via WhatsApp. Both yachts are fully equipped for a day on the water, but the Mangusta offers more built-in excitement.

Which destinations suit each yacht?

Both yachts can reach Marbella's top anchorages: Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, and the coast off Cabopino. The Mangusta 80's speed makes it easier to explore further afield, such as the dunes of Cabopino or the beaches of Estepona. The Canados 86 is better suited for a leisurely day trip to Sotogrande or anchoring near the Marbella Club Hotel for lunch. Both can handle the calm Mediterranean conditions typical of summer.

Pricing and booking

The Mangusta 80 (grey or white) has a fixed price: €4,719 for a minimum of 4 hours, with the jet ski included. The Canados 86 is priced on request — simply send us a message on WhatsApp with your preferred date and duration, and we'll provide a quote. Both yachts are available for half-day and full-day charters, with longer durations negotiable. For other options, check our full fleet.

Which one should you choose?

Choose the Mangusta 80 if you want a sporty, fast experience with a jet ski included, and you're happy with a fixed price for 4 hours. Choose the Canados 86 if you prioritise space, a larger flybridge, and a more luxurious, stable cruise, and are flexible on pricing. Both offer the same inclusions and depart from Puerto Banús. Still unsure? Contact us for personalised advice.

Frequently asked questions

Which is bigger, the Mangusta 80 or Canados 86?

The Canados 86 is larger at 26 metres compared to the Mangusta 80 at 24 metres. Both accommodate 12 passengers, but the Canados offers more deck space and a larger flybridge for entertaining.

Does the Mangusta 80 charter include a jet ski?

Yes, every Mangusta 80 charter (including grey and white variants) includes a free jet ski. This is not included with the Canados 86, though you can request one via WhatsApp.

How much does it cost to charter the Mangusta 80 in Marbella?

The Mangusta 80 costs €4,719 for a minimum of 4 hours. This includes the jet ski, skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% IVA. Longer durations are available on request.

Can I charter the Canados 86 for a half-day trip?

Yes, half-day and full-day charters are available. Pricing is on request — simply message us on WhatsApp with your preferred dates and duration, and we'll provide a quote.

Which yacht is faster, the Mangusta 80 or Canados 86?

The Mangusta 80 is designed for sporty performance and higher speeds, making it a great choice if you want to cover more coastline or enjoy water sports. The Canados 86 prioritises luxury cruising and stability.

Do both yachts depart from Puerto Banús?

Yes, both the Mangusta 80 and Canados 86 depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella. You can also arrange pick-ups from other marinas along the Costa del Sol on request.

What is included in the charter price for both yachts?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden extras.

Which yacht is better for a large group celebration?

Both are excellent for groups of up to 12. The Canados 86 offers more interior and exterior space for socialising, while the Mangusta 80 adds a jet ski for added fun. It depends on your priorities.

Still deciding? Browse our Marbella boat rental options or contact us for a personal recommendation.
